parameters.text:
=You are analyzing a customer support email for InCred Money, a platform for trading unlisted pre-IPO shares.

The email body below may contain a full conversation history as quoted text — read through the entire email including all quoted sections to understand the complete back-and-forth between the customer and support team.

THREAD SUBJECT: {{ $json.subject }}

EMAIL BODY (may contain full conversation as quoted text):
{{ $json.primaryCustomerBody }}

Based on the ENTIRE conversation including all quoted sections, extract TWO types of knowledge:

TYPE 1 — GENERIC KB ENTRY:
A reusable Q&A that answers a common question any customer might ask.
Keep the question generic — no personal details, amounts or order IDs.

TYPE 2 — SCENARIO PATTERN:
How the support team handled this specific type of issue end to end.
Capture the full flow — what customer complained about, what details support asked for, what data was checked, and how it was finally resolved.
Keep situation description generic but keep example_response realistic and specific.

Return ONLY this JSON, no explanation, no markdown:
{
  "kb_entries": [
    {
      "question": "Generic question a future customer might ask",
      "answer": "Complete answer based on how support responded",
      "category": "billing | technical | refund | general_inquiry | complaint | onboarding | liquidation | account",
      "confidence": 0.85
    }
  ],
  "scenario_patterns": [
    {
      "pattern_name": "Short name describing this scenario type",
      "trigger_keywords": ["keyword1", "keyword2"],
      "category": "billing | technical | refund | general_inquiry | complaint | onboarding | liquidation | account",
      "situation": "Generic description of the customer situation — what type of issue they had",
      "response_approach": "Step by step how support handled this — what they asked for, what they checked, how they resolved it",
      "data_to_check": "What to look up — e.g. check transactions table for payment_status, admin_action, nsdl_checked, referral counts etc",
      "example_response": "The actual or closely paraphrased response text the support team sent",
      "confidence": 0.85
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Read ALL quoted sections in the email — the full conversation history is there
- kb_entries and scenario_patterns can each be empty arrays [] if nothing relevant found
- Only include entries with confidence >= 0.7
- If this is an automated notification with no real customer query return {"kb_entries": [], "scenario_patterns": []}
- For scenario_patterns, capture the FULL resolution flow not just the final response

parameters.content:
**KB BUILDER — HISTORICAL EMAILS**

This workflow converts your existing Gmail support inbox into a structured, AI-searchable Knowledge Base. It processes historical email threads end-to-end — fetching, filtering, assembling full conversations, classifying with AI, generating vector embeddings, and writing clean records into PostgreSQL.

The KB it builds becomes the foundation for Workflow 2 (AI Draft Generator), which uses semantic similarity search to pull relevant past Q&A pairs and real support examples every time a new customer email arrives — so AI-generated drafts sound like your team, not a generic template.

Run this workflow manually in batches. Start with limit = 5 to validate the pipeline, then increase to 50 or 100 for bulk import. It is safe to re-run — duplicate detection ensures already-processed threads are silently skipped every time.

**What gets written to the database:**
**kb_data** — One clean Q&A pair per thread. Used in Workflow 2 for semantic KB retrieval.
**scenario_patterns** — One reusable handling pattern per scenario category.
**corrections** — Raw customer message + actual human reply. More examples = better drafts.

**FETCH, FILTER & DEDUPLICATION**

Manually triggered. Fetches the last N emails from Gmail — adjust the limit before each run. Start with 5 to validate, then scale to 100.
Each email is parsed and cleaned — outbound replies, automated senders, and empty bodies are dropped. Checks email_logs for threads already marked kb_extracted and skips them silently.

⚠ Update: YOUR_DOMAIN in the Parse & Filter node · Gmail OAuth2 credential required

**THREAD ASSEMBLY & AI CLASSIFICATION**
Fetches the full Gmail thread and separates customer messages from support replies. Threads with no support reply are dropped — no resolution means no KB value.
GPT-4o-mini then classifies the conversation and extracts a structured JSON output: category, Q&A pair, handling pattern, sentiment, and resolution status. Entries below 0.7 confidence are dropped before proceeding.

⚠ Update: Scenario categories and profile context in the AI node system prompt · OpenAI API key required

**EMBEDDINGS, DEDUP & DB INSERTS**
Generates 1536-dimension vectors via text-embedding-3-small for semantic search. Runs cosine similarity checks before inserting into kb_data and scenario_patterns — threshold 0.92 blocks near-duplicates. corrections insert freely at the same threshold.
Writes sequentially to: kb_data → scenario_patterns → corrections → email_logs.

⚠ Config: Similarity threshold in duplicate check nodes · PostgreSQL + pgvector required
